description_long: This is a wrecked military airplane, partially embedded in a sandy or rocky terrain. The fuselage is mostly intact, displaying prominent 'NAVY' markings and a large star insignia, with the tail section painted in bright orange and white. The left wing is broken and lies flat on the ground, also bearing a star insignia, while the right wing appears to be missing or buried. The cockpit area is exposed and damaged, with internal components visible, and the nose section is heavily deteriorated. The aircraft's tail features a vertical stabilizer with orange coloring and visible serial numbers. The overall surface is weathered, with chipped paint, rust, and debris scattered around the wreck.
description: A wrecked military Navy airplane with a broken fuselage, exposed cockpit, and a partially detached orange-and-white tail, embedded in sandy terrain.
description_view_0: Oblique view showing the broken left wing with star insignia, exposed cockpit, and orange tail section.
description_view_1: Top-down view highlighting the full length of the fuselage, the left wing, and the sandy terrain surrounding the wreck.
description_view_2: Side view displaying the 'NAVY' markings, star insignia, and the mostly intact fuselage with the orange tail.
description_view_3: Rear oblique view focusing on the damaged tail, exposed internal structure, and the left wing lying flat.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Low-wing, straight wings with no visible winglets or pronounced dihedral/anhedral angles; left wing broken and lying flat.
attribute_engine_type: Single piston engine in the nose section, engine components exposed due to damage.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers; vertical stabilizer painted orange.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Tricycle landing gear, partially visible and likely fixed or damaged.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed cockpit with a single canopy, now exposed and damaged.
